title: Components and JSX
description: CReact components are functions that return JSX. JSX describes resources and processes, not DOM elements.
jsx_intro: CReact uses JSX to describe resources and processes, not DOM elements. <WebSite name="blog" /> declares that a website named "blog" should exist.
component_functions_intro: Components are plain functions that return JSX. They receive props as their first argument and run once. Reactivity is handled by signals, not re-renders.
fragments_intro: Use <>...</> to return multiple elements without a wrapper:
reactive_props_intro: Props in CReact are often accessors (getter functions) to preserve reactivity. Components run once; effects re-run when their dependencies change.
tip_access: Use the access() helper to unwrap values that may or may not be accessors: access(props.value) works whether value is 5 or () => 5.
children_intro: Components can receive children through JSX nesting. Use the children() helper to resolve them reactively.
